allies.rationale_text: Cognitive-load-theory is the clearest ally for scaffolding-educational because staged support reduces overload and helps schemas form.
allies.source_quote: This is the most crucial ally. Scaffolding is essentially the practical application of CLT.

allies.rationale_text: Feynman-technique complements scaffolding-educational by forcing the learner to simplify and restate understanding at each stage.
allies.source_quote: The Feynman technique requires the learner to simplify a concept in plain English and use analogies to fill knowledge gaps.

structured_tensions.tension_text: Scaffolding-educational conflicts with first-principles-thinking when rigid instructional order blocks the learner from independently decomposing the problem.
structured_tensions.source_quote: scaffolding-educational conflicts with first-principles-thinking (first principles thinking) when rigid instructional sequence blocks independent decomposition.

structured_tensions.tension_text: Scaffolding-educational conflicts with step-back when too much instructional structure suppresses reflective reframing.
structured_tensions.source_quote: scaffolding-educational conflicts with step-back (step back) when over-structured guidance suppresses reflective reframing.

curation_notes.summary: Preserved only current-vocabulary first-order seams that were explicit or cleanly warranted by the raw source.
donor_drops: Did not keep donor-shaped or out-of-vocabulary relation surfaces; omission beat weak forcing.
open_questions: Higher-order candidates remain observational only and belong in later selective review rather than in this closure batch.

allies: Wave 4 candidacy remains observational only for this model.
antagonists: Any broader antagonistic surface not preserved here was omitted because the current first-order vocabulary did not justify forcing it.
structured_tensions: Only explicit or cleanly source-supported tensions that survive current-vocabulary discipline were preserved.
